\@minji:\ we'll figure it out

Meanwhile, at Sigma Chi, Mark was staring down at his own phone screen. He saw the post between the gym and his shower, and for a few seconds, something hurt in his chest.

It was the thing nobody would ever believe about him, that the flinch came first, every time. He stood in the hallway of Sigma with his towel over his shoulder, looking at Donghyuck folded over his own knees under a flash, and the boy in the photo was suddenly fourteen and asleep on Mark's basement floor with headphones on, sixteen and screaming the bridge of a song they'd wrote but never finished out his broken old car's window. As much as he wanted to forget it, that boy was his oldest friend in the world at his lowest hour, being served up as content, and something in his heart turned over with an ache that was completely, verifiably real.

Still, a sick part of him was almost glad, too. He knew, he knew that Haechan wrecked in public meant Haechan untouchable for weeks, radioactive, done, it meant the last road between Solar and that room was washed out, the lane was clear, all the way to the horizon. Mark hated the count, even as he ran it, but running while hating it had never once stopped him.

But he had a gut feeling that she was looking down at that post with a different feeling than him, that part didn't take intelligence. Solar had a soft heart underneath all the dryness, and the post would sit wrong with her no matter what she currently believed about the boy in it.

And as bad as it was, he knew how to use that to his advantage.

This is a fact that would only become more true as time went on, one that Solar would wish she'd figured out sooner.

Instead, she was a naive girl, one struggling to figure out all of the puzzle pieces so chaotically laid in front of her. It was 7:52 when there was a knock on her door. She was going to ignore it, at first, she was still too deep in her thoughts.

That was, until a voice spoke on the other side, \"Sol, it's a special delivery!\" It was an insistent Hyein, she realized, and there was no helping it.

So, she whipped open her bedroom door to meet Hyein, who was holding a bag of takeout with that big grin on her face. \"Some guy dropped it off at the door,\" she explains, holding the bag out to her, \"and before you ask, yes it's from who you think, and no I didn't touch your fries.\"

The bag was still warm in her hands. Solar pursed her lips as she stared down at it, before looking back up at Hyein and offering a small smile, \"thanks, I\... kinda needed this, to be honest.\"

\"I can tell,\" Hyein sassily retorted, before her eyes widened and she seemed to realize it'd sounded worse than she planned. Clearing her throat, she tries again, \"we're here for you, whenever you're ready to spill the tea,\" she decides, gentler now, \"just know I am very impatient.\"

She chuckled then, \"just give me a while, okay? I'm sorting my thoughts out first.\"

As much as she looked like she wanted to argue, Hyein nods, \"fine, love you!\"

Then she was gone, back to her room where she was probably working on a fashion project. Solar closed her door, looking down at the bag in her hands.

heard mondays are the worst, make sure u eat something. fruits dont count.\ - m

49: everything they once knew

It took Solar the most grueling, endless lecture Tuesday morning to decide that she needed to tell Hanni and Hyein about the Danielle encounter, about the realizations she was coming to, about what she so confidently knew yet couldn't prove. She'd told Minji the day before, and it only felt right that she told her two closest friends, the two people who continued to stand by her side no matter how loud the chaos grew.

She knocked on their bedroom door before she'd even thrown her things in her own room. It was half past lunch, meaning she knew they were in there, likely snacking on fruit while watching Gossip Girl.

That much was confirmed at the sight of Hyein answering the door, a piece of watermelon in her hand half eaten. Her eyes scan Solar's determined expression, registering something before she steps aside without a word, which was perhaps the most serious thing someone like Hyein had ever done.

Hanni was cross legged on her bed with her hair clipped up, a highlighted reading balanced on one knee with her bowl of fruit on the other side of her. The TV was indeed playing an episode of Gossip Girl. Though, at the sight of her friend entering, she'd set the reading aside, eyes immediately attentive with the kindness of a saint. She hadn't said anything either, at first, she just patted the spot next to her, the way you'd coax something skittish out from under a porch.

So she sat, focusing on the plush of the mattress on her skin, letting her bag slide off her shoulder and onto the floor. In return, Hanni wordlessly tilted the fruit bowl towards her, to which she'd shaken her head, her stomach in no condition to be accepting visitors.

The girl just pursed her lips in return, but didn't say anything, she simply looked back to the television. Even Hyein had laid back down on the bed to watch herself, on her stomach with her feet in the air, relaxing like there was nothing else going on in the world. The two were prime actresses, perfectly pretending like they weren't waiting for the inevitable, like they weren't secretly hoping Solar was finally going to let them in on everything that had been going through her head the last week. Of course, they knew something happened, everyone knew something happened. They were also the only people, supposedly, who truly knew that Solar slept with Haechan Lee, that the two of them clearly liked each other, prior to the great shift the campus kept talking about. Everyone loved the rumors, but the two girls were more worried about what that meant for Solar and her mental state. It was clear, though she would never admit such, that what happened with Haechan hurt her, but they didn't have the heart to bring it up.

Though, they were happy that Mark had been there for her, through the thickness of it, trying to lift her back off her feet. The two truly did believe that he liked her, that he wanted the best for her, they didn't think much of it, the way he posted her, not most of the time. Perhaps, it had become so normalized to constantly see Solar's face all over their feeds, that it never read as odd to them, not in that way. Mark was good, they thought, Mark was simple.

But they also knew the weirdness behind SUnews' captions, behind all of the whispers, even if they didn't know the reasons behind it, because like Solar, Hanni and Hyein were freshmen, too. All they really knew, was that Danielle had done things that got a prior Alpha member expelled, resulting in even the leaders of Alpha to be weary of her, so everyone just let her do what she wanted to do. That, and the fact she evidently had a blatant obsession with Jaehyun Lee, and in turn, a heavy dislike for his little sister and her status. But they didn't know the truth of it, they didn't know the eery connection between Danielle and Mark, not yet.

On the screen, her eyes lazily watched Blair continue to wear her facade in a world that was constantly pressuring her from all angles. It twisted something in her chest, the way it felt too familiar, like she was watching an alternate version of herself on a screen, the way she bottled it all up until it shattered, alone in her room.

\"Good episode,\" she mutters, though it was clear her two friends heard her and saw the irony in it, from the way they both exchanged worried glances with one another.

\"A classic,\" Hyein responds, a mumble, anyway.

She watched the show for a few more minutes, growing so engrossed in it that she almost convinced herself she'd only come here to hang out. Just a group of friends, after a boring Tuesday lecture, enjoying each other's company with no other cares in the world. She wished it was true, that she wasn't still lost in her own head about the reality of her life.

Hanni drifted back to her reading, whilst Hyein kept snacking on the fruit at a professional pace, the episode continuing to murmur along. It was somewhere, in the middle of it all, that she blurted out the words, without permission, over the sound of Blair destroying someone's reputation in one of her mood swing manners, \"It's Danielle, you know, I know it for sure\... all year,\" her voice was almost mocking, dry and sucked of flavor, \"can't prove it, but I know it.\"

Hanni accidentally dropped the remote onto the pink carpet beneath them, seeming to forget she was holding onto it. Hyein had to bite her tongue harshly, trying to suppress the sounds she wanted to release. Still, she couldn't seem to help herself as she whispers out, \"God, I really don't wanna say I knew it, but\...\"

\"Then don't, Hyein,\" Hanni hisses at her in disappointment, before turning back to Solar. She reaches out a hand, placing it softly onto her friend's shoulder, \"okay, walk us through it, Sol, what happened?\"

Solar wasn't bothered by Hyein's comment, because she knew she'd known it, too. She just felt like an idiot that she ever doubted herself, it was right in front of her the entire time.

\"The sticky notes,\" she sighs, eyes staring down at her hands in her lap, \"something clicked, the hand writing, how she words things\... think about how the captions sound\... like, cheerful with a knife in it.\"

\"Are we\... sure it's not just a vibe?\" Hanni tries, because she wanted to be optimistic and try to present a scenario where this wasn't happening.

\"I wish,\" Solar deadpans, \"but the way she talks, she'll tell me something then say the same shit reworded slightly days later on the account,\" she scoffs, chuckling in disbelief, \"she's been slowly making it more obvious, just to me\... In our business class, she asked me about Haechan, not because she wanted an answer, but because she wanted to purposely say some things never change before she posted the same thing on the account.\"

Both of their faces fell, growing slightly paler at the mention of the certain boy, at the recollection of the post she was talking about. They knew the post, everyone knew the post, it was plastered everywhere. If you were an SU student, it was highly likely you'd at least heard about it. So, they knew exactly what that caption said, because they'd been sitting in this exact room when they'd read it and made the mutual decision that they shouldn't tell Solar for her own mental sanity.

It was only natural she would find out either way, because Danielle wanted her to see it.

\"Holy shit,\" Hyein gawks, \"she really repeated it word for word.\"

\"Yep,\" Solar exhales, \"and she knew I was the only one who would know, that I can't prove it, so she plastered a photo of him at his lowest with her stupid fucking exclamation marks.\"

Hyein was chewing her watermelon much slower then, in deep ponderance, likely about whatever she was going to claim she would do about this. In reality, there wasn't anything they could do. They were at a dead end with nowhere to go.

\"Okay\...\" Hanni breathes, calmly, after a moment of thought, \"well, you didn't do anything with this information then, right?\"

From the look on Solar's face, Hanni got her answer.

\"Oh shit,\" Hyein snorted, slapping Hanni's arm once she noticed her expression, \"well, can you blame her?!\"

\"I went to her room,\" Solar explains, since the two of them would guess it anyway, \"told her to take it down.\"

\"I\...\" Hanni clears her throat, realizing that she didn't really blame her, either, because it wasn't fair what Danielle was doing, because everything clicked into place so perfectly, \"and\... what happened after that?\"

\"What else could've happened?\" she laughs bitterly, \"She played innocent, acted like I was crazy, called it petty drama\... then when I told her I knew, that I know but I can't prove it, she clicked, showed her true colors,\" she continued, \"and finally, she told me.\"

\"Told you what?\" Hyein was on the edge of the mattress, kicking her feet in desperation to hear the rest of the story.

\"What she knows,\" her voice grew quieter as she recalled it, \"that I\... didn't come back to Alpha that night\... what time I left in the morning\... about the meeting Jaehyun had with them both, which no one other than me should know because I was the only other person Jaehyun told, when he called me and said he handled it, the way he handles everything in my life before asking me,\" she shook her head, \"and she talked about Mark, the whole flowers thing, the bought dinner\...\" she bit down on her bottom lip, trying to piece everything together, the entire mess, to make it make sense in her brain, \"she tried to make it sound like\... she heard it from him.\"

\"Huh?\" Hanni's eyes bulged, \"From Mark?\"

\"Like\... she talks to Mark?\" Hyein repeated, and Solar nodded once, because she couldn't stand to confirm it a second time.

\"But they've never\...\" Hanni trails off, looking like she was going to be sick, \"Right? Those two don't know each other.\"

\"I don't know,\" Solar shrugs, truthfully, \"because she wants to confuse me and get under my skin, so I don't know what's true and what's not\... that's the issue,\" her voice cracks at the end, eyes falling back down to her lap because she didn't want to look like a sad idiot in front of her friends, even though they were the last people who would ever judge her for it. \"Either way, she'll tell Jaehyun if I try anything,\" she finishes, \"about the Sigma party\... about\... Haechan.\"

\"I know,\" Hyein says confidently, trying to lighten the rather dark mood that had polluted the room, \"I'll just kill her! That solves it, no?\"

\"I don't think so, Hyein,\" Hanni softly coaxes.

\"Fine,\" the girl scoffs, \"so, how about putting her spreadsheets in the pool?\"

Solar giggled then, it came up through her chest before she could stop it, watery and involuntary, desperately needed, and Hyein looked quietly proud of herself for having produced it, like it'd been her entire goal from the start. \"I don't think that would do as much as you think.\"

\-

The problem with being told something so heavy at half past lunch is that the rest of the day still has so many hours left in it. Hanni made it through no more of her reading after Solar left, leaving her and Hyein to dwell in everything they just heard. Hyein, in turn, had begun to pace the shared rug in a fury that kept trying to become a plan. The watermelon sat abandoned on the nightstand, and somewhere around the fifth lap of pacing, Hyein's mumbling had turned in coherence that wasn't about Danielle anymore.

\"Mark,\" she announces, \"Mark and Danielle\... talking\...\" She said it, to see how the words tasted on her tongue, and they tasted very odd, but not exactly impossible, which only confused her further.

\"Don't, Hyein,\" Hanni scolded, \"Mark is\... Mark is nice, she was just trying to get under Solar's skin, that's what she does.\"

\"I'm just saying it out loud,\" Hyein claims, \"to see how it sounds.\"

\"He's her friend,\" Hanni corrects, \"he's sweet to her, he likes her, we vouched for him,\" Hanni's voice faltered the more words that came from her lips, and she heard her own sentence land somewhere sour, because vouched was a word you used for things you signed your name to, \"he\...\" she pauses, lips forming a frown, \"he bought the entire team dinner.\"

\"Exactly,\" Hyein agrees, crossing her arms over her chest and turning to Hanni in aching confidence, \"and Sunday\... there were flowers the size of a person, and Monday, there was a Dutch Bros waiting outside her class\... and\... Monday night\... the delivery at our door when she didn't tell anyone about her day\... not even us,\" she taps her foot impatiently, anxiously, \"so, how'd he know, Hanni? You're telling me Mark Lee's a psychic and he only uses it to\... see Solar? I mean, every single time something happens to her, boom, there he is!\"

Now that she said it, it felt far too obvious, and it made a puddle of guilt pool at the bottom of their feet. The gestures lined up in Hanni's memory, exactly the way Hyein was laying them out, a neat little row of kindnesses, each one individually explainable, each one sweet enough to post about. There was a version of that row, where a boy was simply paying attention, and she'd been the loudest defender of that version in the entire house.

But then, there was the other thing, the one that'd been sitting politely at the edge of her mind since half past lunch, waiting for her to be brave enough to look directly at it.

\"Hyein\...\" Hanni starts, her voice small, \"the flowers, they were peonies.\"

Hyein stopped pacing again, \"okay,\" she deadpans, \"and what?\"

\"So,\" she admits, \"those are her favorite, and he knew because he asked me\... all these little questions, like what flowers she likes, if she would like surprises or a warning, her favorite color\... and I\... I answered his questions, because I was so happy about it\... and I stood in our entryway and told everyone he was so thoughtful and kind\...\"

Hyein was standing in the middle of their room now, trying to pick up what Hanni was laying in front of her.

\"So\... if he asks me things and I tell him,\" she continues softly, barely above a whisper, staring at her dried out highlighter, \"and Danielle somehow knows things that nobody tells her\...\" She trailed off, because she didn't have the heart to finish the sentence.

\"Okay,\" Hyein cries, \"okay, but have we ever seen them talk?\"

Hanni had to think about it, honestly and hard, combing back through every instance she had seen Mark and Danielle even near one another, which in her recollection, was only ever at Sigma parties.

\"No,\" she deadpans.

\"Yeah,\" Hyein nods, as if that squished the entire thing, \"exactly.\" She sat down at the desk, with the air of a lawyer that'd won a case she didn't believe in, the room settling into an unquiet quiet, both of them privately reviewing a semester's worth of doorways and deliveries, neither of them wanting to say anything else about it, because they were afraid of the real answer, even if it was laying in front of them in broad daylight.

Though, as they would learn, there was only so much they could make excuses for.

But while the two were busy coming to a conclusion they couldn't deny, Solar was busy avoiding doing exactly that. Instead, she was brainlessly paying attention to the amber lights of the lamps as she walked, where she'd met Minji, at the bottom of the hill by the flickering lamppost, her hands shoved in her hoodie pockets, breath fogging under the buzzing light.

\"Almost two minutes late,\" Minji had shaken her head, \"shame on you, duo.\"

\"You survived, didn't you?\" Solar rose a brow, to which Minji had shrugged.

\"It was a close call,\" she offers into the cold air, after they'd walked for a while.

Solar didn't say anything, she just focused on the scraping of their sneakers against the sidewalk, letting herself enjoy the simplicity, the way that Minji didn't remind her of anyone.

A golden retriever passed them at the corner, dragging a freshman behind it like a parade float, and Minji tracked it the entire way past, her head turning. \"Nine,\" she appraises after the dog was gone.

\"The dog's a ten,\" Solar corrects, \"you know it.\"

\"Point off for the retractable leash,\" Minji counters, \"not the dog's fault, but somebody's gotta be accountable.\"

\"You have a problem,\" Solar giggled.

But Minji waved it off, casually, \"Chenle emailed me the bar graph today, by the way,\" she pivots, \"as a file, subject line, for your records.\"

\"What records?\"

\"You gotta stop asking questions eventually,\" Minji chuckles, \"it's just better that way.\"

She laughed then, and for half a block, things almost felt normal, which was the entire problem. The apology Solar had been drafting in her head since Sunday kept refusing to leave the garage, three separate times she inhaled, ready to let it out, before the sentence would run away somewhere else, the cold, the syllabus, a squirrel with ambitions, and she could tell Minji was aware of it but wasn't going to mention it. She just let her do it, patient as a held shot, which almost made it worse.

That familiar lamppost was long behind them when she finally found the courage.

\"I'm sorry,\" she blurted, \"about\... about Saturday.\"

Minji didn't break stride, like she had no idea what she was possibly talking about, \"which part?\" she asked, which was less of a cruel question and more of a surgical one.

Solar hated that she was making her spell it out, but there was no turning back now. \"The part where I was drunk and\...\" she paused, trying to figure out the right way to say it, \"and\... made you be the responsible one\... it's not fair to you, and it keeps not being fair to you\... and you keep being so normal about all of it like it costs you nothing\... and I\...\" she trails off, because she felt like an idiot and a jerk, all in one, and she couldn't decipher how to get the right words out.

For a few steps, the only sound between them was their shoes against the leaves that littered the sidewalk. Minji was silent, keeping her eyes on the middle distance, working through things privately in her head, the way everything happened, labeling and filing, in the art of a film major.

\"I'm not going to lie to you,\" she states eventually, \"because that's not who I am, so, I'm not going to say it's fine, it didn't mean anything\... or laugh and move on, because one of those things would be a total lie,\" she pauses, opening her mouth before closing it as she picks the proper words, \"but I'm not going to make it a thing, either, this campus has given you nonstop shit, and you what anyone in your position would do,\" she shrugs, \"you\... reached for something warm\... I'm not mad about being warm, Sol.\"

Solar was going to speak, but Minji beat her to it, not done with her speech.

\"I'd just\... rather not be a coping mechanism,\" she finishes, with a lighthearted chuckle, \"that's all, you know, so we're okay, and we'll continue to be that way, so long as you know the difference.\"

She had to let it sit for a minute, though the October air didn't mind one bit. The leaves ruffled in the trees above them, it took the sound for her to finally exhale, \"I know the difference, Ji.\" She realized it was true as she said it, that knowing the difference was an entire separate issue.

\"Cool,\" Minji nods, a single time, and Solar watched her put the whole conversation away somewhere behind her eyes, a set being struck, footage being ejected, and when she turned her head again, she was already someone slightly different, brisker, her captain voice arriving before the words did, \"So, about Danielle\... the accounts.\"

And this, as it turned out, was what saved the evening, because the case was the third thing, a place both of them could safely put their eyes. Solar went through everything again while they walked, the mirror, the phrases, the confrontation, the insinuation about Mark, and Minji listened the way she watched killcams, without blinking, asking exactly the right horrible questions at exactly the right moments.

After Solar finished, only then did Minji ponder aloud, breath fogging ahead of them, \"here's what I know, from the perspective of film,\" she announces dramatically, \"everything leaves footage. She's been running that account for over a year, that's hundreds of posts, you can delete a post, but you can't delete a pattern, and patterns are provable, you know? You put moments next to each other until the meaning becomes undeniable.\"

Solar shook her head, surprised as Minji's knowledgeable read, \"wow, that's\... disturbingly poetic.\"

\"Hey, I'm smart and pretty,\" Minji shrugs, \"so, everytime she says something to you, write it down, date it, time it, whatever. Screenshot everything that account posts so she can't sneakily delete or edit anything, she thinks we have no proof, so we collect until we do. And then\... one day, when it matters, we'll have a supercut instead of a suspicion.\"

Solar blinked at her, at the sheer calm and confidence of a girl that had no reason to help her other than the own kindness of her heart. \"Shit,\" she mutters, \"that's actually so smart of you.\"

Minji laughs then, \"I make things out of footage, it's more dangerous than you'd think.\"

That was how it happened, in the end, nothing planned or decided, just two girls taking the long way on a route her feet knew better than her head did, as the lamps lit their path in the evening night that basked over them. Minji kept gassing herself up, talking about film, they kept devising a further plan to take Danielle down. She didn't mention Mark, the oddness of it, because she could tell that Solar didn't want to face that part, not yet, and she respected that. Because Minji was genuine, in the way that Mark Lee was not.

But her eyes went before her mind could stop them, to the side steps of the building they passed, the Extracurricular Building that should've been nothing more to her than where she played eSports. Though, that spot was engraved, the way certain coordinates simply get downloaded into the brain, the bottom of the side stairs beneath the overhang, where a boy had once sat with a laptop glowing against his face, not a single ounce of performance on it.

And there he was again, like he'd never left those steps.

Her feet didn't stop, some deep survival system kept her them moving, though everything else inside of her stopped entirely. He sat exactly where he'd sat that night, hood up, folded over the laptop with his headphones on, one knee bouncing, the screen throwing that same pale blue up into his face. And his face, his face was doing the thing, the listening harder thing, alive in the specific way it only ever seemed to get about sound. The post said gone, the post said back to who he really is, wrecked, finished, some things never change. Yet, here was a boy at seven in the evening, under the moonlight and amber tones, out in the cold, working like it was all he knew how to do.

That was, until her eyes trailed over the bottles. Not party debris, nothing so deniable, not all the way over here away from the yard of Sigma Chi, standing upright at his feet like they'd been invited, one of the half empty. Even from a distance, she knew that wasn't water, the two facts sat side by side in the pale blue light, refusing to resolve into one boy. The laptop told her the post was a lie, but the bottles seemed to point to something else. He was working, and he was drinking alone in the dark to do it, and there wasn't a caption on this entire campus with room for both of those things being true at once.

It was only four seconds, maybe five, and he never looked up. His head only tilted, some note somewhere not sitting right, his hand moving toward the trackpad, and Solar faced forward and kept walking, heart moving like she'd been sprinting, eyes stinging in a way she immediately and unconvincingly blamed on the wind.

Minji said nothing for ten steps, she'd seen him, of course she had, because she saw everything. That was the terrifying gift about her, and Solar braced herself for the question, the observation, the gentle little scalpel.

\"We can loop the other way next time,\" is all she finally offers, voice perfectly level.

But Solar didn't trust her own voice enough to answer, so she just nodded at the sidewalk instead. Neither of them said a single word about bottles, or frequencies, or who the supercut was truly for.

\-

The doorbell rang at 6:55 PM, and Hanni and Hyein were the only ones downstairs to hear it, or so they believed.

Both of them had migrated to the kitchen around six, under the official flag of making dinner, which was really just stewing with extra steps, and Hyein had been mid quesadilla flip when the bell went. Later, they'd remember that neither of them moved, not right away, some new house instinct making them look at one another first.

By the time they'd reached the hallway. someone else's heels had already crossed the entryway, the front door wide open. The two stopped where the hallway met the entry, barely outside of the sightline, in the shadow the coat rack threw against the wall. They hadn't planned to eavesdrop, that was the thing they'd keep insisting after the encounter, the way you press on a bruise to check it's still there. There was simply no version of moving that wasn't louder than staying still.

\"\...already told you, she's not here,\" Danielle's voice echoed through the room, entirely bored, \"you have her number, don't you? God, be less obvious.\"

\"Yeah\... no, I know,\" whoever was standing at the door returned, though the familiar voice caused both of their eyes to widen. There was a shuffle of weight on the porch boards, before Mark's voice dropped lower, into something that had no dorky wave in it whatsoever, no granola bar, a register neither girl had ever heard him use in their lives, \"look, forget it, it's fine\... but\... that thing we talked about, that's still good, yeah?\"

That thing they talked about.

There was a pause, exactly the length of a raised eyebrow.

\"I don't know what you're referring to, Mark,\" Danielle replied pleasantly, followed by a deep sigh, like she was losing precious time.

\"Right,\" a short laughed followed, one that had nothing funny in it, \"yeah, obviously\... my bad.\"

\"Kay, goodnight, Mark,\" Danielle yawns, fakely, her voice still light, effortless, like the conversation was already over for her, \"you know where to find me.\"

The porch boards shifted again, restless, and when he spoke next, it was quieter, half to her and half to nobody, the sound of a boy talking himself into his own harmlessness, \"it's not a big thing, Danielle\... I just want an in, that's all this ever was.\"

\"I get it,\" she returns flatly, \"though, maybe check your best friend's reasoning, too, yeah?\"

Her heels turned then, clicking away, and in the half second before Danielle's shape came back around the corner, Hyein hauled Hanni bodily into the kitchen doorway. The two of them arranged themselves around the pan, into a diorama of girls who'd absolutely been cooking this whole time, hearts hammering like rabbits. Danielle passed the kitchen then, without so much as a glance, because she never needed to glance, and went up the stairs at her usual administrative pace.

But the front door of Alpha Phi was still open, with Mark standing there in the porch light, in deep thought, hands in his jacket pockets. When he noticed the two slowly emerging into the entryway, his smile was already arriving before anything else could, the way weather arrives.

\"Oh, hey guys,\" he offers a single wave, \"the door was, uh\... I was in the area, figured I'd come say hi to Sol, she around?\" His hand came up to scratch the back of her neck, the whole dorky assembly reconstructing itself in real time, piece by piece.

The two were very quiet. Hanni's typical brightness didn't arrive the way it would've otherwise, the sizzling sound of their food echoed from the kitchen.

\"She's with Minji,\" Hanni answers finally, her voice finding it's normal tone and keeping it before she could say anything else she'd regret.

\"Oh, right, no, that's good,\" he nods, a few too many times, \"that's good for her, just\... tell her I stopped by, or, actually don't\... it's not\... not a big deal, for real,\" he smiled again, stuttering over his words in a way they would usually guess was because he was Mark Lee, the awkward, dorky frat boy that people expected to stumble. The two friends watched it, the way you watch a magic trick, after someone's told you where the card goes, \"night guys\... food smells great, by the way.\"

The door shut behind him with a click, leaving Hyein and Hanni standing there, neither of them saying a thing. Mark's footsteps went down the porch stairs, unhurried, a boy with nowhere urgent to be.

They went back into the kitchen in silence. Hyein turned off the stove, watching the food continue to sizzle in the hot pan.

\"Hyein\... he said\...\" Hanni started, before she trailed off, because her heart didn't want her to say the words aloud.

\"Uhuh\...\"

\"He talks to her,\" Hanni mumbles, \"the thing they talked about\... you know where to find me\...\" her whisper was coming apart, at the edges, \"I\... I gave him her favorite color\... I told him about the peonies\...\" she turned to Hyein with glassy eyes, clearly torn apart by this terrible news, by the horrible reality the two girls were facing. Hanni felt guilty, she felt entirely guilty for contributing to the hurt that her beloved friend had been going through, that a boy she saw as entirely innocent was actually the complete opposite. \"What\... what has she been giving him?\"

Hyein stared through the kitchen doorway, at the closed front door, and for once in her entire life, she took her time before speaking. And when she finally did, there wasn't a single ounce of caps lock left in her voice.

\"We can't tell Solar,\" she decides, \"not tonight, not until\... we have something real,\" she turns to Hanni confidently, \"we can't do that to her\... she found out her own sorority sister runs a smear machine and got blackmailed for it all before lunch\... she can't\...\" her voice breaks, \"she can't find out that\... whatever he is\... is doing whatever this is, not on the same day, Hanni\... it'd break her.\"

Hanni frowned, shaking her head. She knew Hyein was right, that it was the rare moment of smart thinking you'd only get under special circumstances from her. Solar had been holed up in her room the entire week, clearly down, hurt, and stressed. If they told her this, it would only make it all worse.

Little did they know, she'd already begun to suspect it herself.

\"So what do we do?\"

\"What she can't do,\" Hyein gently pried the spatula from Hanni's hands, setting it down on the counter, \"watch him. Every gesture, delivery, every single time he knows something he shouldn't. Starting tonight, nothing that boy does around her is cute anymore,\" she states, \"it's data,\" she exhales, half in quiet pride that she had kept her cool, and half in sadness that this was actually happening, \"God, I can't believe I didn't see it sooner.\"

\"I know,\" Hanni whispers, \"me too.\"

Hyein was tapping her foot impatiently on the tiled floor, still running through everything in her head, thinking back to watching Mark and Haechan that night at the Sigma party.

\"So\...\" she seems to realize, \"do you think\... Haechan\...\"

\"I don't think anything now, Hyein,\" Hanni pouted, entirely confused on everything she once thought she knew.

50: something is off with mark lee

Hanni had been awake for twenty minutes before she finally gave up on pretending otherwise, rolling onto her back and staring at the pale ceiling above her bed. Across the pink carpet, Hyein sat at their desk, shaded by the blue light of her laptop, still wearing the oversized shirt she'd slept in, one foot tapping against the carpet at a speed that suggested she hadn't gotten much sleep, either.

Neither of them said a word, at first. There were too many things waiting to be said, yet every single one seemed to begin with Mark standing beneath the Alpha Phi porch light, his hands in his jacket pockets, rebuilding his smile in front of them.

I just want an in.

Hanni pulled her blanket higher over her chest, but the warmth did nothing. She couldn't stop seeing the flowers in her own hands, the cream paper, the peonies she'd told him about with such ridiculous excitement. She'd thought, she was helping someone love her friend correctly, but then, every answer she'd given him felt like a door she'd held open without checking who was really standing on the other side.

At the desk, Hyein's tapping stopped.

\"That's it, I'm asking Haechan,\" she announced, firmly, matter of factly, entirely decided.

Hanni turned her head on the pillow, eyes bulging, \"No!\" she cried immediately, refusing to even think about such a possibility.

The girl at the desk, however, was offended. \"You didn't even think about it,\" she sighed in disappointment.

\"I have been thinking about everything since last night!\" Hanni snapped, though it wasn't mean, it was exasperated, stressed.

Hyein pushed away from the desk, the wheels of the chair catching on a shirt she'd left on the floor. She leaned down to yank it free before spinning to face Hyein properly, gathering one knee against her chest.

\"Exactly,\" she insists, keeping her voice low enough that it wouldn't travel beneath the door, \"and none of it makes sense, so, why don't we ask the one person who would obviously know?\"

\"And what are we even going to ask?\"

Such a question, Hyein hadn't prepared for. \"Don't know yet,\" she deadpans, but her confidence didn't falter despite the admittance, \"but it will be something!\"

\"Hyein\...\"

\"What?!\" her bare foot returned to the carpet, and she was tapping again, anxiously, determined. \"We're sitting here acting like Mark being weird automatically means Haechan is good, and I know it doesn't, but what if half the reason we think Haechan is bad is because Mark kept telling everyone he was?\"

On the surface, her words sounded crazy, were Hanni's first thoughts.

But then, she let it sit, abruptly sitting up, gathering her blanket around her waist.

Meanwhile, Hyein was busy tapping away on her keyboard.

DINNER\ PHOTO\ FLOWERS\ COFFEE\ FOOD DELIVERY\ DANIELLE???

\"Hyein, we know what people say about him,\" Hanni reminds her, gently, \"it isn't only Mark.\"

\"Yeah,\" Hyein nods, flatly, sassily, \"and now we know who runs the account that makes sure everyone keeps saying it.\"

A silence fell over them. Hanni looked down at her hands, thumb rubbing slowly over the edge of the blanket, remembering the night of the Sigma party; the way Mark arrived at the couch looking like he'd misplaced something precious, Solar texting them from upstairs, insisting Haechan was kind of nice, in a weird way.

She recalled her own words to Solar, that she'd never heard Haechan treat another girl the way he treated her, if he seems genuine, maybe they shouldn't worry so much about the rumors.

Then he'd disappeared, and Mark was there, telling Solar that's just how he was, that he was playing a game. But it didn't look like a game he'd played before.

And they'd sat there and let him.

\"What would we even say?\" she finally sighs.

Hyein's expression brightened before she'd managed to contain it, placing both hands on the arms of the chair, leaning toward Hanni, \"we tell him she's hurt.\"

Hanni looked unsure, \"we don't tell him anything she gave us in confidence.\"

\"Fine,\" Hyein nods in agreement, \"that's fine, we tell him\... what anyone with eyes can see! She's been hiding in her room, she keeps saying she's fine, like she gets paid every time she lies\... oh, and every time he's mentioned, she looks like somebody kicked her.\"

\"That's a little dramatic.\"

\"No, it's accurate,\" she monotones.

Hanni frowned at her, but the argument had nowhere to go, Hyein was right. Even before Solar told them about Danielle, before the relapse post and confrontation, they'd watched her fold in around the particular absence Haechan left behind. Nonetheless, Mark filled it with coffee, flowers, carefully timed food, but filling something wasn't the same as making it disappear.

Still, there was another truth sitting beside it, one Hanni refused to put away simply because Mark had become harder to trust.

\"But we don't know Haechan,\" she states with an exhale, \"not really, we don't know what he wanted from her, and we don't know if he'll tell us the truth.\"

\"Okay, then we watch him lie,\" Hyein decides confidently, before her voice seemed to falter. Her eyes look down at her hands, picking at the chipped polish on one thumbnail, and her voice was barely above a whisper when she spoke next, \"at least it'll be him doing it this time.\" The sentence landed heavier than she appeared to expect, her face changing after the words left, confidence bending into something more careful.

Seeming to come to terms with it, Hanni lets out a deep breath, reaching for her phone on the nightstand. \"We ask him ourselves,\" she affirms, \"that's all, we don't promise anything to Solar, we don't tell her what we think, and if he acts like he doesn't care, we leave him alone.\"

Hyein was already turning back toward the desk, failing to hide the satisfaction creeping across her face. \"So, Halloween then.\"

\"So, nothing yet,\" Hanni corrects.

\"He'll be there,\" she assures her, \"obviously!\"

\"You don't know that,\" the angelic girl snaps, \"you know you can't trust what you see on SUnews.\"

\"True,\" Hyein pouts, before a light bulb seemed to appear over her head. \"I've got it!\"

She picked up her phone before she could change her mind, tapping into her recent Instagram followers. It didn't take her long to find a certain name, a new friend she had assuredly made at the last party they attended at Sigma, when everything was less chaotic.

Solar kept her phone beside her notebook, half concealed beneath the edge of her the desk. Solar pressed her lips together, fighting the smile threatening to appear. The girl beside her glanced over, prompting her to lower the brightness of her screen.

She'd had enough of Sigma to last her through graduation. There wasn't a single part of the house she could think about without something else arriving behind it, the kitchen, the upstairs hallway, the side gate Mark used to let her in, the bedroom that smelled like vanilla and faint smoke, the perfectly calibrated headphones. Even the living room had become Jaehyun's meeting and Danielle's information.

But going back felt like knowingly stepping into a room after hearing something break inside of it.

\@hanni:\ and you shouldnt have to miss halloween bec things have been difficult

Solar read the messages again. No boy drama, Hyein promised, which was a sentence with very little historical evidence behind it.

Still, staying inside Alpha Phi on Friday somehow felt worse. She could already picture herself sitting on her bed while music traveled faintly across campus, checking accounts she swore she wasn't checking, allowing Danielle, Mark, Haechan and her brother to take turns deciding where she was allowed to exist.

And like always, Solar Lee never turned down a challenge.

The professor stopped speaking, causing Solar to slowly look up, only to find him staring toward the back rows, one hand still raised toward the graph.

Every heard between them turned, girls looking at her with slightly glad smiles. She locked her phone, straightening in her seat, composure sliding over her face by instinct, \"of course not.\"

\"Then perhaps, you'd like to rejoin us.\"

He held her gaze for another second before continuing the lecture. Solar waited until his back was turned, before her eyes locked back onto the screen, looking down at the final message.

\-

By seven Wednesday evening, Hyein had turned the Alpha Phi living room into a costume department run by someone with no budget restrictions and very limited adult supervision. Three shopping bags sat open across the coffee table, surrounded by jewelry, makeup, reference photos, and a roll of gold ribbon Hyein continued moving place to place without ever using. Hanni sat cross legged on the floor with a small sewing kit, repairing one of the transparent wing straps that had arrived broken.

Meanwhile, Solar stood in front of the mirror, wearing an orange satin top and a green skirt Hyein acquired without consulting her. It seemed, that she'd had this planned for a while.

The skirt was undeniably short.

Not the shortest skirt she had ever seen worn at a Sigma party, not even close, but short enough that Solar had spent several minutes pulling it down, only to discover it returned to the same place the moment she moved.

\"I'm not wearing this,\" she deadpans, feeling far too uncomfortable in her skin recently to dare make such a decision.

Hyein, kneeling behind her with several gold accessories looped around one wrist, looked personally wounded. \"What, why?!\"

\"If I bend over, the entire house is going to learn things about me.\"

\"Okay,\" she returns blankly, \"don't bend over, simple.\"

\"Fantastic solution,\" Solar sarcastically remarks, throwing her hands in the air in exasperation, turning away from the mirror to glare down at her friend. Hyein reached forward, shrugging carelessly, to adjust one of the gold chains at her waist, as if the argument had already been resolved.

That was, until the doorbell rang, echoing through the house, causing everyone to halt their actions.

\"I'll get it,\" Hanni offers, placing the repaired strap carefully on the table before rising to her feet.

Solar, shamelessly, attempted to follow Hanni, a last ditch effort at escaping this dungeon she was trapped in. But Hyein caught her wrist, lecturing, \"you're not done, ma'am!\"

\"I'm trying to go greet my friend,\" Solar complained, almost in a whine.

\"She knows what you look like.\"

\"Unfortunately.\"

Hyein sent Solar an unimpressed look, before she continued to fasten a gold cuff around Solar's upper arm, when Hanni re-entered the living room with Minji trailing behind her.

As per usual, the camera bag hung lazily over one shoulder, with both of her hands tucked into the pockets of a SU hoodie. She stopped at the entrance to the living room, leaning against the archway for a moment, taking in the scattered clothes, glitter, and Hyein kneeling at Solar's feet.

Only then, did she exhale deeply into the momentary silence, \"I've made a grave mistake.\"

\"You've been here four seconds,\" Solar deadpans.

\"And, I'm a quick study, Sol.\"

Hyein, being the concentrated one for once, rose and crossed the room, gently gripping Minji's shoulders and steering her toward the couch before she could object, \"no time for chit chat, we need to try yours.\"

\"Gee, hello to you, too,\" Minji mutters, but didn't make much of an effort to leave, despite having every chance to do so.

\"Hi,\" Hyein monotoned, before shoving a folded blue top into Minji's hand, \"hold that.\"

Feeling helpless, Minji's eyes trailed across the room, where Solar was still standing by the mirror with Hanni busily trying to place the wings on her back. Her eyes trailed across her body, seeming to download the sight of the girl, before she met Solar's irises, raising a brow, as if asking for assistance.

Solar pursed her lips, ignoring the heat she could feel under her cheeks. \"Um, I promised to protect the cargo pants,\" she calls to Hyein, innocently.

Hyein, who had been nearly buried deep in heaps of fabric, looked up at the sound of these words. Her brows furrow for a second, in deep thought, before she nodded a single time, \"I can do that.\"

Minji looked between the two, seemingly pleased with this outcome, \"the alliance stands.\"

And for the next hour, the room remained in constant motion.

Hyein adjusted colors, rejected jewelry, and stood several feet away from them with narrowed eyes, like she was judging a gallery installation. Hanni moved between the two girls with safety pins held carefully between her lips, fixing straps and smoothing fabric, a true assistant, as Hyein called her. Minji refused glitter three separate times, before Hyein caught her distracted by Solar and brushed it across one of her cheeks anyway.

The dark haired girl recoiled, one hand flying to her face, \"did you just touch me with that?\"

\"You moved,\" Hyein shrugs, before she moved onto the next task like nothing happened.

A look of defeat took over her face, as she turned toward the mirror, inspecting the shimmer across her cheekbone, \"I feel\... violated,\" she mutters.

\"You look beautiful,\" Hanni assures her, reaching around to straighten the strap at Minji's shoulder.

\"Those aren't mutually exclusive.\"

Solar giggled from where she sat on the couch, attempting to fasten the strap of her wings that Hanni hadn't tightened quite enough. The clasp slipped from her fingers for the third time, before she'd moodily whined out, \"okay, someone help.\"

Hanni and Hyein hadn't moved quick enough, because in seconds, Minji was standing in front of her. The two girls noticed her movement, and let it happen, standing there to watch the exchange with small grins on their faces.

For a moment, Solar could feel the warmth of her hands without being touched by them. She wasn't looking at Solar as she did it, her brown eyes were intently focused on the clasp, or at least she was making it seem like they were. It took her a second to get it, her fingers grazing across the surface of her collarbone as she did so. Something almost caught in Solar's throat, before she hurriedly reminded herself of the conversation they'd had, of her own reality, and pushed it away.

\"There,\" Minji murmurs, stepping away once the strap was secure, \"not entirely structurally sound, but good enough for Sigma.\"

Hyein, meanwhile, stepped back to study the three of them. She'd assigned herself Bloom, naturally, with Hanni as Flora, Minji as Musa, and Solar being given Stella before she had any chance to object, because apparently, being named Solar also meant surrendering all costume autonomy.

\"This\... is perfect!\" Hyein squeaks, hands clasped beneath her chin, \"everyone is hot, the theme is obvious, and the wings are small enough that even Solar can't complain!\" She started clapping and jumping around a few times, dreamily looking up at the sky, \"oh, Mr. T would be so proud of me.\"

\"I can easily complain anyway,\" Solar announces.

\"That is true,\" Hanni agrees warmly.

\"So, what's the actual plan Friday?\" Minji asked, because no one else had mentioned it, and she was still shocked that Solar agreed to do such a thing.

Hanni and Hyein exchanged another glance, before Hanni began, \"we go together, stay together, have fun\... and if anyone feels uncomfortable, we leave together, no questions asked.\"

The four were standing in front of the mirror now, squished together, staring at their own reflections.

\"No boy drama!\" Hyein cheers.

Solar desperately wanted it to be true, so much so that she mustered the courage to nod, \"no boy drama.\"

Her phone buzzed on the coffee table behind them. The girls didn't seem to notice, too focused on talking about their costumes, the way that Minji pulled off blue well, and Solar casually slipped away to pick up the device.

\@markylee:\ hey sol

\@markylee:\ hanni said ur coming fri

\@markylee:\ any costume spoilers?? :)

Solar blinked down at the screen, something in her brain seeming to alert her that something didn't feel right. Hanni said, the words repeated, her eyes dragging upwards across the room, where Hanni was helping Minji remove one of her silver cuffs with the focus of a parent.

As soon as she'd gotten it off, a victory smile spread over her lips, until she'd seemed to feel Solar's eyes. \"What?\" she offers, confused.

\"Did you tell Mark we're going on Friday?\"

She'd sat the cuff on the coffee table, the metal hitting the glass with a click. \"No,\" she shakes her head slowly, \"I haven't talked to Mark today, Sol.\"

Hanni seemed to be rather confused on why Solar was asking such a thing, or she was acting far too well for Solar to notice otherwise. The girl's eyes fell back on her screen, leaving enough time for Hanni to turn to look at Hyein with wide eyes. Minji met their gaze, too, seeming to inform them with her eyes that she was also aware of what was happening.

But no one wanted to say it.

\"What does that mean?\" Solar was muttering under her breath as she stared down at the screen.

\"Well,\" Hanni clears her throat suddenly, seeming to recall something, \"he did ask me over the weekend, about Halloween, if you liked dressing up\... I told him Hyein was trying to plan something, but I didn't know what it would be yet.\" The guilt arrived across her face before she'd even finished speaking. Though, even then, it didn't seem to make sense, the way he knew what he knew. Hanni and Hyein knew it as she said it, something was still clearly off, but the last thing Hanni would do was lie.

Individually explainable, Solar pondered, that was the problem.

\@sollee:\ promise is a strong word

She read the question again, not because it was difficult to understand, but because she could hear the hope tucked beneath how casually he'd asked it. Mark had been kind to her all week, whatever else Danielle meant, whatever she was implying about him, Solar didn't know enough to convict someone for being kind at the right times. She didn't know if she still believed the words Haechan kept telling her, the scholarship story, the he does it on purpose, because Haechan was seemingly nowhere to be seen, all because of what?

Her brother, who he had only ever ignored before, or Mark?

Yet, the problem was still clear. The flowers were still beautiful after she heard the receipt underneath them, the food had still been warm when she needed it, Mark could be genuine and calculating at the same time. And she was beginning to fear that every person on campus contained two facts nobody was willing to place besides each other.

\@markylee:\ word ;)

\-

Hanni and Hyein were in the living room when Solar returned from Thursday practice, the day after the girls tried on their costumes. The only thing she'd really had to worry about that day was her practice, since Thursdays were her easy day of the week. Still, there was something evident laying on her shoulders as she entered the living room to see her friends lounging there, beneath the same blanket with a bowl of popcorn between them. Hyein looked up the moment the front door had opened, eyes moving over Solar's practice bag and flushed cheeks, presumably from her walk home with Minji, a quiet tradition that had begun and no one else questioned.

\"How was practice?\"

\"Painful,\" the girl dramatically returns.

\"Sounds like character building,\" Hanni offers.

Solar leaned against the doorframe with her arms crossed, informing, \"Minji's coming at seven thirty tomorrow.\"

\"Perfect,\" Hyein clapped her hands together, \"I'm doing makeup!\"

\"That's fine with me,\" Solar shrugs, \"but Minji might be a different story.\"

\"Well, good thing I'm not asking.\"

Solar chuckled, pushing away from the doorframe before turning towards the stairs, too tired to have much more of a conversation, calling behind her, \"goodnight, guys.\"

\"Goodnight, Sol!\" the two called back in unison.

They waited until the sound of her bedroom door closing upstairs echoed faintly. Before them, the television continued to play, its light moving silently over the living room walls. Hyein reached beneath the blanket for her phone, opening the note she'd began that morning.

Beneath the list of Mark gestures, she typed a final line.

FRIDAY - ASK HAECHAN

Hanni was leaning over her shoulder, watching her thumbs fly across the screen, the popcorn forgotten in her lap.

\"That's all,\" she reminds her friend.

Though, neither of them sounded convinced.

But they knew it had to happen, because something was off with Mark Lee.

And they were going to get to the bottom of it.
